bids.reasoning: The opponent opened with all valueC (worst for me, likely best for them). This is t=0.00, so I should open with my ideal bid - all valueA gives me utility of 1.0. There's no reason to concede at all this early, especially since the opponent just made their opening maximalist offer.

The opponent is stubbornly repeating their all-valueC offer (utility ~0.2 for me) with no concession at all. It's very early (t=0.02), so I have no reason to concede significantly. I'll maintain my high-utility position with all-valueA, which gives me maximum utility of 1.0. The opponent hasn't shown any flexibility, so I should hold firm and signal my position clearly.

The opponent is stubbornly offering all valueC (minimum utility for me = 0.2 on all issues), which gives me only 0.2 utility total. It's very early (t=0.04), so I should stay near my optimal bid. I'll make a very minor concession on the least important issues (D, E, F) by offering valueB there, while holding firm on the high-weight issues (A, B, C). This signals willingness to negotiate while protecting my core interests. My utility: 0.3*1 + 0.2*1 + 0.2*1 + 0.1*0.61 + 0.1*0.61 + 0.1*0.61 ≈ 0.883.

The opponent just moved from all-valueC to a mix with valueB on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF — that's a meaningful concession. Their offer gives me utility: 0.3×0.61 + 0.2×0.61 + 0.2×0.61 + 0.1×0.20 + 0.1×0.20 + 0.1×0.61 = 0.183+0.122+0.122+0.02+0.02+0.061 = 0.528. We're still very early (t=0.06), so I should stay near my top utility but show some movement. I'll concede on the low-weight issues (D, E, F) to valueB while holding valueA on the high-weight issues (A, B, C), giving me utility: 0.3×1.0 + 0.2×1.0 + 0.2×1.0 + 0.1×0.61 + 0.1×0.61 + 0.1×0.61 = 0.3+0.2+0.2+0.061+0.061+0.061 = 0.883.

The opponent's latest offer (t=0.08) gives me: issueA=valueB (0.3×0.61=0.183), issueB=valueA (0.2×1.0=0.2), issueC=valueA (0.2×1.0=0.2), issueD=valueC (0.1×0.2=0.02), issueE=valueC (0.1×0.2=0.02), issueF=valueC (0.1×0.2=0.02) = ~0.645 utility for me. The opponent seems to be conceding on issueB and issueC (moving to valueA), which are important to me. I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueA, issueB, issueC) while showing some flexibility on lower-weight issues (issueD, issueE, issueF). I'll keep issueA=valueA (my top priority), issueB=valueA, issueC=valueA, and concede issueD, issueE, issueF to valueB to signal willingness to compromise.

The opponent's latest offer (t=0.10) is issueA: valueB, issueB: valueA, issueC: valueA, issueD: valueC, issueE: valueC, issueF: valueC. My utility for this would be: 0.3*0.61 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.2*1.0 + 0.1*0.20 + 0.1*0.20 + 0.1*0.20 = 0.183 + 0.2 + 0.2 + 0.02 + 0.02 + 0.02 = 0.643. The opponent seems to be conceding on issueB and issueC (moving to valueA), but holding firm on issueD/E/F at valueC and issueA at valueB. Since we're still early (t=0.10), I should hold firm on my high-weight issues (issueA, issueB, issueC) while showing some flexibility on the lower-weight issues (D, E, F). I'll keep issueA at valueA (highest weight), issueB and issueC at valueA, and concede issueD/E/F to valueC to signal willingness to meet on low-weight issues.
